summary: Tested and fixed the three-dot menu button (⋯) in PostModal mobile view. The menu button was working in Playwright tests but user reported it not working on real mobile devices. Applied fix to prevent backdrop from intercepting touch events on the top bar area.
action_items: Test on real mobile device to confirm fix works - Playwright tests pass but real mobile touch events may behave differently
critical_code_review_comments: Root cause identified: The tap-to-close backdrop (inset-0 bottom-[200px]) was covering the top bar area where the menu button is located. Even though z-index was correct (backdrop z-1, top bar z-10), touch events on mobile may have been intercepted.
Fix applied: Changed backdrop from 'inset-0 bottom-[200px]' to 'inset-0 top-[60px] bottom-[200px]' to exclude the top bar area
Added data-testid='post-modal-menu-button' for better test targeting
Added onPointerDown with stopPropagation to prevent event bubbling
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fixed PostModal mobile menu button. The issue was that the tap-to-close backdrop was covering the top bar area. Changed backdrop from 'inset-0 bottom-[200px]' to 'inset-0 top-[60px] bottom-[200px]'. Playwright tests confirm menu opens with 7 items (Report, Add to favorites, I was there too, About this account, Go to post, Share to..., Copy link). Real mobile device testing recommended.

fix_details.problem: Three-dot menu button (⋯) in PostModal mobile view not responding to taps on real mobile devices
fix_details.root_cause: The tap-to-close backdrop div had 'inset-0 bottom-[200px]' which covered the entire top area including the menu button. While z-index was correct (backdrop z-1, top bar z-10), touch events on mobile may have been intercepted by the backdrop.
solution: Changed backdrop from 'inset-0 bottom-[200px]' to 'inset-0 top-[60px] bottom-[200px]' to exclude top bar area
